§ 4 The provisions of the caput of this article apply to data mining, by public or private entities, in the context of AI systems to combat civil and criminal offenses, which violate copyright and related rights.

paragraphs: Art. 64. The holder of copyright and related rights may prohibit the use of content owned by them in the development of AI systems in cases not covered by art. 63 of this Law.

Single paragraph. The prohibition of the use of protected works and content in the databases of an AI system subsequent to the training process does not exempt the AI ​​agent from being liable for moral and material losses and damages, in accordance with applicable legislation.

paragraphs: Art. 65. The AI ​​agent who uses copyrighted and related content in mining processes, training or development of AI systems must remunerate the holders of this content due to this use, and must ensure:

I – that the holders of copyright and related rights have effective conditions to negotiate collectively, in accordance with Title VI of Law No. 9,610, of February 19, 1998 (Copyright Law), or directly the use of the contents of which they are the holders, being able to do so free of charge or for a fee;

II – that the calculation of the remuneration referred to in the caput considers the principles of reasonableness and proportionality and relevant elements, such as the size of the AI ​​agent and the competitive effects of the results in relation to the original content used;

III – free negotiation in the use of protected content, aiming to promote a research and experimentation environment that enables the development of innovative practices, and that does not restrict the freedom of agreement between the parties involved, in accordance with arts. 156, 157, 421, 422, 478 and 479 of Law No. 10,406, of January 10, 2002 (Civil Code), and art. 4th of Law No. 9,610, of February 19, 1998 (Copyright Law).
§ 1 The remuneration referred to in the caput of this article is only due:
I – to holders of national or foreign copyright and related rights domiciled in Brazil;

II – to people domiciled in a country that ensures reciprocity in the protection, in terms equivalent to this article, of copyright and related rights of Brazilians, as provided in arts. 2nd, sole paragraph, and 97, § 4th, of Law No. 9,610, of February 19, 1998 (Copyright Law), charging being prohibited in cases where reciprocity is not assured.

§ 2º The holder of the remuneration right provided for in the caput who opts for direct negotiation and authorization, under the terms of item I of the caput, will be able to exercise it regardless of subsequent regulation.

Art. 66. The use of image, audio, voice or video content that portrays or identifies natural persons by AI systems must respect personality rights, as provided for in Law No. 10,406, of January 10, 2002 (Civil Code), and in the relevant legislation.

paragraphs: Art. 67. Sectoral authorities must define different criteria for AI systems offered by micro-enterprises, small businesses and startups that promote the development of the national technology industry.
Single paragraph. Differentiated criteria must consider the competitive impact of related economic activities, the number of users affected and the nature of the economic activities carried out.

paragraphs: Art. 68. The following constitute guidelines for the actions of the Union, the States, the Federal District and the Municipalities in the development of AI in Brazil:

I – establishment of multi-participatory, multi-sector transparent, collaborative and democratic governance mechanisms, with the participation of the government, the business sector, the third sector and the academic community, especially considering vulnerable groups;
II – promoting trust in AI technologies, with the dissemination of information and knowledge about their ethical and responsible uses;
III – encouraging training and preparing people for the restructuring of the job market;

IV – promotion of interoperability between AI systems, including between different federal levels and different sectors of society, with decisions that seek to avoid technological dependence and promote the continued use of developed or contracted systems;
V – advertising and dissemination of data, in an open, structured and secure manner;
VI – environmental protection and sustainable development;
VII – promoting international cooperation, by encouraging the sharing of knowledge about AI systems, in order to facilitate regulatory and technological interoperability;

VIII – promotion of investment in AI aimed at solving the country's problems, promoting its socioeconomic, cultural and environmental development, and for the development of the national and regional production system, valuing the country's technological autonomy and its insertion and competitiveness in the domestic and international markets.
Art. 69. AI systems of public authorities must seek to:

I – accessibility of people, regardless of their physical-motor, perceptual, sensory, intellectual, mental, cultural and social capabilities, safeguarding aspects of confidentiality and administrative and legal restrictions;
II – compatibility with both human reading and automated processing of information;
III – ease of use of e-government services that use AI systems;
IV – guarantee of transparency regarding the use of AI systems;
V – promotion of Portuguese culture and language;
VI – encouraging the development of national AI systems, with special attention to national issues and cultural, language and socioeconomic context nuances.
Art. 70. The public administration, within the scope of the Union, the States, the Federal District and the Municipalities, will implement programs to:
I – education, training, training, technical and higher qualification and requalification in AI aligned with the demands of the market and the public sector;
II – digital literacy for meaningful, responsible and equitable use of available AI systems, prioritizing basic education;

III – support for workers impacted and possibly affected by the adoption of AI, with a focus on promoting well-being, requalification, adaptation to new job market demands and professional reintegration;
IV – awareness and training in sustainability in the field of advanced digital technologies, with an emphasis on responsible practices in the use of resources; and

V – encouraging educational institutions to include subjects on environmental impact and sustainability in the development and operation of AI systems and applications and other advanced digital technologies in their curricula.
§ 1 The programs referred to in the caput will seek to reduce inequalities, especially between the different regions of the country.

§ 2 The literacy measures referred to in section II of the caput will include basic notions and skills about AI systems and their functioning, including the different types of products and uses, their risks and their benefits.
Art. 71. The State must periodically formulate and promote studies, as well as set goals, strategies, plans and schedules, relating to the use and development of AI in the Country.

paragraphs: Art. 72. The rights and principles expressed in this Law do not exclude others provided for in the national legal system or in international treaties to which the Federative Republic of Brazil is a party.

paragraphs: Art. 73. In order to promote national technological development, the SIA will regulate simplified regimes, involving relaxation of regulatory obligations provided for in this Law, in the following cases:
I – open and free standards and formats, with the exception of those considered high risk or that fall under Section V of Chapter IV of this Law;
II – national promotion;

III – encouraging innovation and scientific and technological research in the productive environment, with a view to technological training, achieving technological autonomy and the development of the country's national and regional production system;
IV – projects of public interest and those that meet the priorities of industrial and science, technology and innovation policies and that are related to solving Brazilian problems;

V – projects carried out in public-private partnerships, or in strategic partnerships, in a Scientific, Technological and Innovation Institution (ICT), Technological Innovation Center (NIT), support foundation, technology parks or technology hubs.
Single paragraph. The Executive Branch will provide economic incentives in the cases provided for in the caput of this article.
Art. 74. In accordance with Chapter IX of this Law, the Executive Branch:

I – provide, within 2 (two) years, the necessary resources to the ANPD, including for its administrative restructuring, in order to guarantee legal certainty and efficiency in the supervision and inspection of compliance with this Law;
II – define the list of bodies and entities that will appear as sectoral authorities that are part of the SIA;
III – will define the detailed composition of Cria;
IV – will define the activities of the Federal Public Administration Mediation and Conciliation Chamber; and
V – will define the composition and powers of Cecia.
Art. 75. The caput of art. 3rd of Law No. 14,533, of January 11, 2023 (National Digital Education Policy), comes into force with the addition of the following item VI:
VI – critical algorithmic literacy and critical computing, which involves reading about the social and human implications of technologies, especially considering artificial intelligence.
